title: Venezuelan Quesadilla
description: A twist on the classic Mexican dish, this Venezuelan version adds a smoky and spicy kick. Perfect for a quick and easy dinner.

ingredients.name: smoky cheese
ingredients.notes: Monterey Jack or Pepper Jack work well

ingredients.name: spicy sauce
ingredients.notes: Salsa Roja or hot sauce of your choice

ingredients.name: vegan cheese
ingredients.notes: Optional, for an extra creamy filling

ingredients.name: caramelized onions
ingredients.notes: Cooked with a bit of oil until sweet and tangy

ingredients.name: diced tomatoes
ingredients.notes: Fresh or canned, whichever you prefer

ingredients.name: chopped cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh for a burst of flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Add a pinch to bring out the flavors

instructions: Preheat a large skillet or griddle over medium heat.
In a bowl, mix together the cheese, spicy sauce, and vegan cheese (if using).
Place one quesadilla in the skillet and sprinkle a quarter of the cheese mixture onto half of the tortilla.
Add a spoonful of caramelized onions, diced tomatoes, and chopped cilantro on top of the cheese.
Fold the tortilla in half to enclose the filling.
Cook for 2-3 minutes or until the tortilla is crispy and the cheese is melted.
Flip the quesadilla and cook for an additional 2 minutes or until the other side is also crispy.
Repeat with the remaining quesadilla and filling.
Cut into wedges and serve hot with your favorite sides, such as salsa or guacamole.
